seoTitle: South Tammin vs South Trayning — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Tammin and South Trayning. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Tammin vs South Trayning: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Tammin and South Trayning.
heading: South Tammin vs South Trayning — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Tammin, Western Australia and South Trayning, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Trayning has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to South Tammin (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Tammin or South Trayning safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Tammin has a flood risk score of 44/100, while South Trayning scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Tammin scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. South Trayning scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Tammin or South Trayning?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Trayning has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
